title: rubus moluccanus var. pilocalyx
intro: Rubus moluccanus var. pilocalyx, also known as the Moluccan raspberry or Mountain raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to make herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and supporting the immune system. Additionally, the plant's fruit and leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including diarrhea, dysentery, and respiratory infections.
